message:
feat(core): add native LLRB tree for search.h port (bd-srch-1)

First sub-bead of bd-srch-epic (port POSIX search.h to native Rust).
The previous tsearch family in frankenlibc-abi/src/search_abi.rs uses
an unbalanced BST with O(n) worst case. This commit adds a clean-room
left-leaning red-black tree (Sedgewick LLRB) in
frankenlibc-core/src/search/ that gives guaranteed O(log n) and is
the foundation for subsequent sub-beads to wire tsearch/tfind/tdelete/
tdestroy into.

Public API (no unsafe — core enforces #![deny(unsafe_code)]):
- RbTree::{new, len, is_empty, insert, find, delete, walk,
  destroy_with, max_depth}
- RbWalkOrder::{InOrder, PreOrder, PostOrder}
- Generic over K with caller-supplied comparator on each op
  (so abi can adapt opaque *const c_void keys)

LLRB invariants enforced via fix_up + rotate_left/right + flip_colors
+ move_red_left/move_red_right.

11 unit tests pass:
- empty_tree_basics, single_insert_then_find,
  duplicate_insert_returns_false
- ascending_inserts_stay_balanced (depth ≤ 22 for n=1024)
- random_order_inserts_stay_balanced (depth ≤ 32 for ≤2048 keys)
- inorder_walk_yields_sorted
- delete_existing_returns_key, delete_missing_returns_none,
  delete_all_keeps_balance
- destroy_with_callback_visits_all, destroy_empty_safe

Closes bd-srch-1; bd-srch-2 will wire the abi layer.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+//! POSIX `<search.h>` data structures and algorithms.
+//!
+//! This module is a clean-room native Rust port of the binary-tree
+//! (`tsearch`/`tfind`/`tdelete`/`twalk`/`tdestroy`), hash-table
+//! (`hcreate`/`hsearch`/`hdestroy`), and linear-search (`lsearch`/
+//! `lfind`) families. The previous fl-abi implementation used an
+//! unbalanced BST with O(n) worst case; this module uses a left-
+//! leaning red-black tree (LLRB, Sedgewick 2008) for guaranteed
+//! O(log n) insert/find/delete.
+//!
+//! No `unsafe` code: keys are stored by value and the public API
+//! takes a comparator closure on each call so the data structure
+//! can be reused with arbitrary opaque key types from the abi
+//! layer (typically `*const c_void` adapted via a wrapper).
+
+pub mod rb_tree;
+
+pub use rb_tree::{RbTree, RbWalkOrder};
